list.name: SR
list.comment: The SR bistable is a latch where the Set dominates.

list.name: RS
list.comment: The RS bistable is a latch where the Reset dominates.

list.name: SEMA
list.comment: The semaphore provides a mechanism to allow software elements mutually exclusive access to certain resources.

list.name: R_TRIG
list.comment: The output produces a single pulse when a rising edge is detected.

list.name: F_TRIG
list.comment: The output produces a single pulse when a falling edge is detected.

list.name: CTU
list.comment: The up-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached a maximum value.

list.name: CTU_DINT
list.comment: The up-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached a maximum value.

list.name: CTU_LINT
list.comment: The up-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached a maximum value.

list.name: CTU_UDINT
list.comment: The up-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached a maximum value.

list.name: CTU_ULINT
list.comment: The up-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached a maximum value.

list.name: CTD
list.comment: The down-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached zero, on counting down from a preset value.

list.name: CTD_DINT
list.comment: The down-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached zero, on counting down from a preset value.

list.name: CTD_LINT
list.comment: The down-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached zero, on counting down from a preset value.

list.name: CTD_UDINT
list.comment: The down-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached zero, on counting down from a preset value.

list.name: CTD_ULINT
list.comment: The down-counter can be used to signal when a count has reached zero, on counting down from a preset value.

list.name: CTUD
list.comment: The up-down counter has two inputs CU and CD. It can be used to both count up on one input and down on the other.
list.usage: (BOOL:CU, BOOL:CD, BOOL:R, BOOL:LD, INT:PV) => (BOOL:QU, BOOL:QD, INT:CV, R_TRIG:CD_T, R_TRIG:CU_T)

list.name: CTUD_DINT
list.comment: The up-down counter has two inputs CU and CD. It can be used to both count up on one input and down on the other.
list.usage: (BOOL:CU, BOOL:CD, BOOL:R, BOOL:LD, DINT:PV) => (BOOL:QU, BOOL:QD, DINT:CV, R_TRIG:CD_T, R_TRIG:CU_T)

list.name: CTUD_LINT
list.comment: The up-down counter has two inputs CU and CD. It can be used to both count up on one input and down on the other.
list.usage: (BOOL:CU, BOOL:CD, BOOL:R, BOOL:LD, LINT:PV) => (BOOL:QU, BOOL:QD, LINT:CV, R_TRIG:CD_T, R_TRIG:CU_T)

list.name: CTUD_UDINT
list.comment: The up-down counter has two inputs CU and CD. It can be used to both count up on one input and down on the other.
list.usage: (BOOL:CU, BOOL:CD, BOOL:R, BOOL:LD, UDINT:PV) => (BOOL:QU, BOOL:QD, UDINT:CV, R_TRIG:CD_T, R_TRIG:CU_T)

list.name: CTUD_ULINT
list.comment: The up-down counter has two inputs CU and CD. It can be used to both count up on one input and down on the other.
list.usage: (BOOL:CU, BOOL:CD, BOOL:R, BOOL:LD, ULINT:PV) => (BOOL:QU, BOOL:QD, ULINT:CV, R_TRIG:CD_T, R_TRIG:CU_T)

list.name: TP
list.comment: The pulse timer can be used to generate output pulses of a given time duration.

list.name: TON
list.comment: The on-delay timer can be used to delay setting an output true, for fixed period after an input becomes true.

list.name: TOF
list.comment: The off-delay timer can be used to delay setting an output false, for fixed period after input goes false.

list.name: RTC
list.comment: The real time clock has many uses including time stamping, setting dates and times of day in batch reports, in alarm messages and so on.

list.name: INTEGRAL
list.comment: The integral function block integrates the value of input XIN over time.

list.name: DERIVATIVE
list.comment: The derivative function block produces an output XOUT proportional to the rate of change of the input XIN.

list.name: PID
list.comment: The PID (proportional, Integral, Derivative) function block provides the classical three term controller for closed loop control.
list.usage: (BOOL:AUTO, REAL:PV, REAL:SP, REAL:X0, REAL:KP, REAL:TR, REAL:TD, TIME:CYCLE) => (REAL:XOUT)

list.name: RAMP
list.comment: The RAMP function block is modelled on example given in the standard.

list.name: HYSTERESIS
list.comment: The hysteresis function block provides a hysteresis boolean output driven by the difference of two floating point (REAL) inputs XIN1 and XIN2.
